tweets.text: .@POTUS: “Meeting the moment on climate change must begin with a recognition that every nation has a responsibility and every nation is at risk.” More: https://t.co/7Igaq19LYQ. https://t.co/8l5OtzUzS2

.@ClimateEnvoy: The biggest step of all this decade is scaling up the development of a global clean energy economy. Energy accounts for three-quarters of global greenhouse gas emissions.The good news is that proof is all around us that we can do it. https://t.co/iWFYLIwWap https://t.co/orDLRdKlgw

The United States is leading international COVID-19 pandemic response efforts. Through #COVAX, we are donating 5,660,460 doses of the Pfizer COVID-19 vaccine to the people of South Africa in collaboration with @gavi and the @_AfricanUnion. https://t.co/ZwMM1TiKVv
.@SecBlinken: “We must recommit to our alliances – and to the shared values that sustain them.” Read more: https://t.co/zpl6j2k8eB. https://t.co/Eb6EgLb5cf

We commemorate our 232nd anniversary this week and recognize the extraordinary efforts of our employees, who work with our partners and allies to promote democracy, peace, and prosperity. https://t.co/6vV53sDkDL

The United States has delivered millions of COVID-19 vaccination doses in an effort to get safe and effective vaccines to as many people around the world as fast as possible. Read more about our deliveries here: https://t.co/MVy4OEA5st. https://t.co/8TpoXVd5Do
.@SecBlinken on support for #OperationAlliesRefuge: We’re working to make good on our commitment to those [Afghans] who helped us and put their lives on the line https://t.co/UQaOyabKVc

.@POTUS on the Afghan Special Immigrant Visa program:  Our message to those women and men is clear: There is a home for you in the United States if you so choose and we will stand with you, just as you stood with us. #OperationAlliesRefuge https://t.co/AYPuGHoRqY

We are proud to partner with our friends and allies to end the global COVID-19 pandemic. Today's delivery of 1.5 million doses of Pfizer’s safe and effective vaccine to Thailand helps us achieve that objective, together. https://t.co/Cnsa2IBcLt

The United States is committed to defeating the global COVID-19 pandemic alongside our international partners. Today, we are proud to announce the delivery of one million doses of the Moderna vaccine to Tunisia. https://t.co/Ded6oPsW2e

The United States is proud to donate more than one million doses of the Johnson & Johnson vaccine to the people of Cambodia. These vaccines are part of our commitment to bring an end to the COVID-19 pandemic. https://t.co/NDGDwQGwYU

.@SecBlinken: We must intensify our efforts and revitalize our role as a leader in the global fight to end human trafficking, reaffirming our pledge to engage meaningfully with survivors and combat human trafficking in all its forms. #EndHumanTrafficking https://t.co/RiztQ7r54P

It is the responsibility of the international community to work together toward the shared goal of preventing and combating human trafficking, protecting victims, and empowering survivors. https://t.co/k0pqeKEqeB #EndHumanTrafficking https://t.co/afjI6fpXXD

The United States is working with the Uzbekistan government to deliver three million doses of the Moderna COVID-19 vaccine to the people of Uzbekistan. Together, our global vaccination efforts can save lives and bring an end to the pandemic. https://t.co/UcTJ5mUVTf

We are pleased to announce the 2021 #IndoPacificBizForum on October 28-29. Attendees may engage virtually with the U.S. and #IndoPacific governments and business leaders on economic recovery, climate action, and digital innovation. Registration opens soon. https://t.co/IVv8K26EAq

On #NationalInternDay, one State Department intern reflects on her experience and on diplomats past and present working to create a more democratic and peaceful world. Read more ⬇️. https://t.co/8gKj9lYcPA

It's #NationalInternDay! Here at the Department we value our interns and appreciate their hard work and dedication in assisting to upkeep the Department's mission. If you are interested in becoming an intern at the Department, visit https://t.co/CqRHMQ9GyE. https://t.co/2pgRnqGWYm
On #NationalInternDay, learn how students from @StKate and @virginia_tech partnered with the State Department to explore agricultural solutions to the climate crisis. https://t.co/F8oPqJHZqa

Working with our African partners to stop COVID-19 is a top priority. We are proud to announce the donation of an additional 151,200 doses of the Johnson & Johnson vaccine to Niger via @gavi, the @_AfricanUnion, and #COVAX. https://t.co/0ouAhYQ2rE

The second U.S. shipment of three million Moderna COVID-19 vaccine doses were delivered to Pakistan today, adding to our previous delivery of 2.5 million doses. We will continue to do all we can to vaccinate as many people as possible with a singular objective of saving lives. https://t.co/gXXsoWzMvd

Throughout the pandemic we wanted to still give students a chance to work with us through our virtual internships. Check out our latest DipNote from an @NMADmuseum virtual intern and read all about her experience. https://t.co/O39vsNVkRD

This year, the United States and Kuwait mark 60 years of diplomatic ties. Our two countries continue to strengthen cooperation on defense and security, trade and investment, customs and border protection, consular services, human rights, and educational and cultural exchanges. https://t.co/DJ07nVbTuS

Kuwait has provided generous support to the COVAX Facility, underscoring the important role Kuwait plays in advancing humanitarian support in the region and helping to end the pandemic. https://t.co/XyvgFXl6sL https://t.co/0qOZGLDkTA
Kuwait is a major non-NATO ally of the United States. Kuwait and the United States are parties to a bilateral Defense Cooperation Agreement. https://t.co/XyvgFXCHRl https://t.co/Auvzm2ToS4

Congratulations to @StateDeptDSS' Benjamin Sides for being named a 2021 Employee of the Year by CAREERS & the disABLED Magazine for his advocacy efforts on behalf of people with disabilities in the workplace and in the community. https://t.co/xTJNCXuoeH

Meet the 2021 #P3ImpactAward finalists! Announced by @GPatState, along with @ConcordiaSummit and @DardenMBA, this annual award recognizes exemplary public-private partnerships providing solutions to pressing issues around the world. https://t.co/CKuG8GOuls

.@SecBlinken: We’ll address the current emergency while also taking the long view. We can’t just end this pandemic. We must also leave our country and the world better prepared for the next one. The United States will rise to the challenge. #VaccinesWork https://t.co/H2fvmYcJIs

The U.S. continues to work closely with India to prepare for and respond to the ongoing pandemic by: bolstering COVID-19 vaccination & the oxygen supply; strengthening detection; training health care workers; developing & producing new vaccines, therapeutics, and diagnostics. https://t.co/n9x4U3NPji

The United States will continue to work with India and Quad partners to advance coordination on development assistance, post-pandemic economic recovery, and combating climate change while promoting prosperity, security, and rule of law throughout the Indo-Pacific. https://t.co/NP2soV8KWn

The United States looks forward to leading with India on addressing the climate crisis, including strengthening cooperation to meet the goals of the Paris Agreement and helping each country achieve its respective climate and clean energy goals. https://t.co/tPiOQn3c5k https://t.co/rOB0T8C2uN

The U.S. stands with India as they continue to battle COVID-19. The U.S. has contributed more than $200M for India's COVID-19 relief and response efforts, including more than $50M in emergency supplies and training, benefitting more than 43M people. https://t.co/tPiOQn3c5k https://t.co/0ADATXtGun

Through @FeedtheFuture, the U.S. government’s global hunger & food security initiative, the United States is working with our partners across all sectors around the world to address the root causes of poverty, hunger, & malnutrition and to transform lives. https://t.co/oQmEyvDaTZ

Our top priority is to keep the world safe from COVID-19. In collaboration with @gavi, the @_AfricanUnion, and #COVAX, we are donating an additional 151,200 doses of the Johnson & Johnson vaccine to Zambia. https://t.co/Ty6LTDD14u

As the U.S. continues to donate vaccines around the world, we are proud to send a second shipment of one million Pfizer vaccines to Paraguay. Only by getting vaccines to people can we help defeat the pandemic. https://t.co/KsqJ5dBvUV
Today’s designations underscore the Biden-Harris Administration’s commitment to promoting accountability for abuses against the Syrian people. https://t.co/JHmJWYEwEc

On the 70th Anniversary of the @UN Refugee Convention, we remain committed to supporting refugees and vulnerable populations globally, ensuring they can build new lives in safety and security. https://t.co/Kt0YRRFWWA

The U.S. continues to support the people of Somalia. $199 million in new humanitarian assistance will provide critical aid to refugees and displaced persons facing chronic food insecurity, violence, and other humanitarian and environmental challenges. https://t.co/GNW3Js1yFY
.@SecBlinken: I am in New Delhi to underscore the importance of our relationship and to continue to deepen our ties and expand cooperation. https://t.co/nnT6MdFlY2

Working with our global partners to stop COVID-19 is a top priority. We are proud to announce the donation of 302,750 doses of the Johnson & Johnson COVID-19 vaccine to Madagascar via @gavi, the @_AfricanUnion, and #COVAX. https://t.co/HPpqZcyZ3p

Our top priority is keeping everyone safe from COVID-19, and we can help end the pandemic by working together with our global partners. .@POTUS on COVID-19: We're going to fight this virus with equity - equity for all. https://t.co/wpltgfGjT0

The United States’ COVID-19 vaccine strategy is a vital component of our global strategy to lead in defeating this and future pandemics. We will be an arsenal of vaccines for the world.  Read more about our strategy for global vaccine sharing here: https://t.co/IZTDC3gUPF https://t.co/ZahF5mVpIx

The United States is proud to donate three million more doses of the Moderna COVID-19 vaccine to Vietnam. We continue making progress toward our commitment to make the world more secure against the threat of COVID-19. https://t.co/uambowvgiF

Today marks the 31st anniversary of the Americans with Disabilities Act—the U.S. civil rights law ensuring that all persons with disabilities have the same rights as others. Through diplomacy, we promote the ADA’s mandate of ​inclusion globally. https://t.co/f4xBFGC1Ve

The United States is committed to providing safe and effective U.S. COVID-19 vaccinations for our partners around the world. Through our partnership with @gavi and the @_AfricaUnion, we delivered 302,400 doses of the Johnson & Johnson vaccine to our friends in Liberia. #COVAX https://t.co/FkenqZNyoc

.@SecBlinken: Standing for people’s freedoms and dignity honors America’s most sacred values. At our best, we stand for freedom and justice for all. Not just here at home, but around the world. https://t.co/fdFjb23NJc

.@SecBlinken: @POTUS has committed to putting human rights back at the center of our foreign policy, and that’s a commitment I and the entire Department of State take very seriously. https://t.co/hVtEk5NknU

.@SecBlinken participated in the International Religious Freedom Summit, where he discussed our commitment to promote and defend religious freedom around the world for all. "Freedom of religion or belief is a human right." https://t.co/QUlbj5vUGu

.@SecBlinken: Religious freedom is a key element of an open and stable society. Without it, people aren’t able to make their fullest contribution to their country’s success. And whenever human rights are denied, it ignites tension, it breeds division. https://t.co/jC8hs4ApWw

.@SecBlinken: Religious freedom is a human right; in fact, it goes to the heart of what it means to be human – to think freely, to follow our conscience, to change our beliefs if our hearts and minds lead us to do so, to express those beliefs in public and in private. https://t.co/xVQuduOGtG

The United States is leading international COVID-19 pandemic response efforts. Today, we delivered 3.5 million more doses of the Moderna vaccine to Colombia. We are committed to ending the pandemic by sharing safe and effective vaccines. https://t.co/KUR1Y3J6Zm

Today’s donation of 302,400 Johnson & Johnson COVID-19 vaccine doses to Morocco through #COVAX and the @_AfricanUnion demonstrates the U.S. commitment to helping the global community end the pandemic. https://t.co/6AHsg8KYya

.@SecBlinken: Religious freedom, like every human right, is universal.  All people, everywhere, are entitled to it no matter where they live, what they believe, or what they don’t believe. https://t.co/HRUUAIqgzs

.@ClimateEnvoy: We can – and must - achieve this together – especially knowing the triumph or tragedy of the two alternative worlds that await our choices. Glasgow is the place, and 2021 is the time. In little more than a hundred days, we can save the next hundred years. https://t.co/o1aN2tJdc9

.@ClimateEnvoy: The suffering of COVID-19 will be magnified many times over in a world that does not grapple with and ultimately halt the climate crisis. We do not have the luxury of waiting until COVID-19 is vanquished to take up the climate challenge. https://t.co/darIJd8bdK

Will you be watching the Olympic Games this year? It has been 5 years since the last summer Olympics, and our U.S. athletes are ready. Check out the latest ShareAmerica article, featuring a few of the more than 600 U.S. athletes to watch. #Tokyo2020 https://t.co/MtEjnAILK4

The Olympic and Paralympic Games bring the world together in sport and remind us of our shared humanity. We join our nation in wishing @TeamUSA the best of luck as they compete in the #TokyoOlympics. #SportsDiplomacy https://t.co/eJJ2zK7FK1

This week the U.S. & Rwanda virtually hosted @UN Member States to prepare for the December 2021 UN Peacekeeping Ministerial. Capacity-building partnerships strengthen performance & help peacekeepers protect communities affected by conflict. #PKMinisterial https://t.co/AF9Nsa9MQ1

Working with our partners @gavi, #COVAX, and the @_AfricanUnion, the United States is donating 151,200 doses of the Johnson & Johnson COVID-19 vaccine to Niger. This is another step of our effort to donate approximately 25 million vaccine doses to AU member states. https://t.co/hxvmXFAeb2

#TIPReportHeroes: Rocío Mora-Nieto, for her efforts to combat the sexual exploitation of women and sex trafficking in Spain. Her work has helped provide those with lived experience of human trafficking access to shelters and care centers, legal advice, and psychological support. https://t.co/ztigSoFYQ8

#TIPReportHeroes: Mohammed al-Obaidly initiated many programs that have had significant impacts for trafficking victims & provided greater protection for migrant workers. The U.S. appreciates his contribution to Qatar’s recent progress and to the important work that lies ahead. https://t.co/S9d3R8bght

#TIPReportHeroes: Guillermina Cabrera Figueroa prosecutes human trafficking cases in Mexico. In addition to her anti-trafficking accomplishments as a prosecutor, she has a track record of championing the rights of trafficking victims and assisting those most in need. https://t.co/MPg3i9DuWH

#TIPReportHeroes: The Department recognizes Shakhnoza Khassanova for her work in Kazakhstan to #EndTrafficking. She raises awareness about human trafficking, advocates for greater victim protections, and aides migrants and victims of human trafficking from across Central Asia. https://t.co/Mj19Jmu8pP

In our continued fight against the COVID-19 pandemic, today we sent the first million of two million doses of the Pfizer vaccine committed to Ecuador. The United States' goal is to equitably maximize the number of safe vaccines for the greatest number of countries. https://t.co/GgYGLyaJNM

#TIPReportHeroes: The Department recognizes Shoichi Ibusuki. He has been an unrelenting champion of protecting foreign workers’ rights in Japan, and he has worked tirelessly for years on behalf of foreign technical trainees to assist victims of forced labor and prevent abuse. https://t.co/ccnxCUdxHb

#TIPReportHeroes: The Department recognizes Chantal Sagbo Sasse. She has been at the forefront of Gabon’s anti-trafficking movement for more than two decades, launching one of the country’s preeminent NGOs dedicated to combating child trafficking. https://t.co/XPqKUi7wNp

#TIPReportHeroes: The Department recognizes Josiane Lina Bemaka-Soui. She has developed the Central African Republic’s national anti-trafficking response from the ground up and led the implementation of the country’s first national action plan on human trafficking. https://t.co/5SZq8cJYG9

#TIPReportHeroes: The Department recognizes Sister Imelda Poole. She has prioritized achieving systemic change through grassroots action and is persistent in advancing advocacy, outreach, and rehabilitation services to combat human trafficking. https://t.co/qWheCkNEw0
